m_Item1.QuestionText: The goal of hashing is to produce a search that takes ___________ time.
m_Item1.Explanation: Hash table (or Hash Map) is a data structure that can map keys to values.A hash table uses a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets, from which the desired values can be found.Time complexity of a well defined Hash function can be O(1).

m_Item1.QuestionText: The goal of hashing is to produce a search that takes ___________ time.
m_Item1.Explanation: Hash table (or Hash Map) is a data structure that can map keys to values.A hash table uses a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets, from which the desired values can be found.Time complexity of a well defined Hash function can be O(1).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Given an array arr = {5,6,77,88,99} and key = 88; How many iterations are done until the element is found in Binary Search?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Binary Search, as the elements are in sorted order, getting through the middle element on first iteration, we find 77. As the key 88>77, we should move towards the right part to 77on next iteration, in which we get immediately 88. On comparing the key 88 to the element we have got in the iteration 88, they are matched. So, on Iteration 2, the element is found.

m_Item1.QuestionText: A technique for direct search is
m_Item1.Explanation: Hashing is used to index and retrieve items in a database because it is easier to find the item using the shortened hashed key than using the original value.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The searching technique that takes O (1) time to find a data is
m_Item1.Explanation:
• Hashing is a well-known technique to search any particular element among several elements.
  • It minimizes the number of comparisons while performing the search.
  • Hash value of the data item is then used as an index for storing it into the hash table.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
If h is any hashing function and is used to hash n keys in to a table of size m, where n<=m, the
expected number of collisions involving a particular key x is :
m_Item1.Explanation: A situation when the resultant hashes for two or more  data elements in the data set U, maps to the same location in the has table, is called a hash collision.  If key is X then number of collisions are less then 1

Consider an example of hash table of size 20, and the following items are to be stored. Item are in the (key,value) format.

Options.OptionText: A function has allocated memory to keys
A function that computes the location of the key in the array
A function that creates an array

m_Item1.Explanation: In hashing Hash function is use to generate hash key is location where you need to insert element in array .

m_Item1.QuestionText: In chaining, what data structure is appropriate?
m_Item1.Explanation: chaining is just like as sequence and addition and deletion becomes easy in singly linked list so signly linklist is appropriate data structure for chaining.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following  has search efficiency of O(1)
m_Item1.Explanation:
Hash Table is a data structure which stores data in an associative manner. In a hash table, data is stored in an array format, where each data value has its own unique index value.
So search efficiency is O(1).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a technique to avoid a collision?

Options.OptionText: Make the hash function appear random

m_Item1.Explanation: On increasing hash table size, space complexity will increase as we need to reallocate the memory size of hash table for every collision. It is not the best technique to avoid a collision. We can avoid collision by making hash function random, chaining method and uniform hashing.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Consider a hash table of size 7, with hash function H(k)=k%7 ,and pseudo random i=(i+5)%7. We want to insert the following keys one by one from left to right.

15 , 11, 25 , 16 , 9 , 8, 12.
 what will be the position of the key 25, if we use quadratic probing?
m_Item1.Explanation:
1) 15  so (15%7)=1 insert 4 at 1
2) 11 so (11%7)=4 insert at 4
3) 25 so (25%7)=4  //collision
   i=4
  i=(i+5)%7 //using quadratic probing.
  i=(4+5)%7=2
 hence 25 shifted to 2nd position.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following trait of a hash function is most desirable

Options.OptionText: it should be easy to implement

m_Item1.Explanation: Hash function calculates and returns the index for corresponding data.So the most important trait of a hash function is that it should cause a minimum number of collissions.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the variant of hash table has the best cache performance?

Options.OptionText: Hash table using linked list for separate chaining.
Hash table using search tree for separate chaining.
Hash table using linked list for separate chaining.

m_Item1.Explanation: Implementation of the hash table using open addressing has better chache performance as compared to chaining .It is because open addressing stores data int the same table without using any extra space.

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Given the following input (4322, 1334, 1471, 9679, 1989, 6171, 6173, 4199) and the hash function x mod 10, which of the following statements are true?

i. 9679, 1989, 4199 hash to the same value
ii. 1471, 6171 hash to the same value
iii. All elements hash to the same value
iv. Each element hashes to a different value
m_Item1.Explanation:
Hash function given is mod(10).
9679, 1989 and 4199 all these give same hash value i.e 91471 and 6171 give hash value 1

m_Item1.QuestionText: Consider a hash table of size seven, with starting index zero, and a hash function (3x + 4)mod7. Assuming the hash table is initially empty, which of the following is the contents of the table when the sequence 1, 3, 8, 10 is inserted into the table using closed hashing? Note that ‘_’ denotes an empty location in the table.
m_Item1.Explanation:
hashing and probing. Let us put values 1, 3, 8, 10 in the hash of size 7. Initially, hash table is empty
    - - - - - - -
    0 1 2 3 4 5 6
The value of function (3x + 4)mod 7 for 1 is 0, so let us put the value at 0
    1 - - - - - -
    0 1 2 3 4 5 6
The value of function (3x + 4)mod 7 for 3 is 6, so let us put the value at 6
    1 - - - - - 3
    0 1 2 3 4 5 6
The value of function (3x + 4)mod 7 for 8 is 0, but 0 is already occupied, let us put the value(8) at next available space(1)
    1 8 - - - - 3
    0 1 2 3 4 5 6
The value of function (3x + 4)mod 7 for 10 is 6, but 6 is already occupied, let us put the value(10) at next available space(2)
    1 8 10 - - - 3
    0 1 2 3 4 5 6

m_Item1.QuestionText: To retrieve a value stored in a hash table.

Options.OptionText: Do a linear search on the table
Do a binary search on the table.
Hash the key and then locate the associated record.
Construct a binary search tree from the table and search the tree.

m_Item1.Explanation:
Hashing is implemented in two steps:
An element is converted into an integer by using a hash function. This element can be used as an index to store the original element, which falls into the hash table.
The element is stored in the hash table where it can be quickly retrieved using hashed key.
